_comment: This is the MCP server catalog template. Place at ~/.agents/mcp-settings.json. NEVER commit real API keys — use ${ENV_VAR} references. Manage keys via PQC secrets (see docs below).

tavily-search.description: Web search and research via Tavily API

brave-search.description: Web search, image search, news, and LLM context via Brave Search API

firecrawl-mcp.description: Web scraping, crawling, search, and agent research via Firecrawl

browsermcp.description: Browser automation — navigate, click, type, screenshot

chrome-devtools.description: Chrome DevTools — performance traces, network inspection, Lighthouse audits

context7.description: Up-to-date documentation and code examples for any library or framework

gitnexus.description: Code knowledge graph — query, impact analysis, Cypher queries, rename

_security_guide.title: Security-First API Key Management with PQC
_security_guide.principle: Zero plaintext keys on disk. All API keys encrypted at rest via ML-KEM-768 + AES-256-GCM. Keys loaded in-memory at shell startup, never written to project configs.
workflow: 2. Pack API keys: pqc-secrets pack < keys.txt → ~/.config/pqc-secrets/secrets.bundle.json
3. Load at shell startup: secrets-load (evaluated by .zshrc / .bashrc)
4. Catalog uses ${ENV_VAR} refs — no raw keys anywhere
5. Pull into project: .agents mcp pull brave-search → .mcp.json has ${BRAVE_API_KEY}
6. Agent reads env var at runtime — key never touches disk
_security_guide.env_var_flow: ~/.config/pqc-secrets/secrets.bundle.json → secrets-load (decrypt in-memory) → export BRAVE_API_KEY=BSAGXd... → ${BRAVE_API_KEY} in catalog → .mcp.json with ${BRAVE_API_KEY} → agent reads from env
